Precinct torii |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Atami|Q653364|WD=Q653364|1=Atami}}Kamitaga 741-1 |位置 = {{coord|35|3|43.8|N|139|4|15|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}{{ill|lt=candidate shrine|Q135022904|WD=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = July 28-29 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=Taga Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|3|43.8|N|139|4|15|E}}}} }} '''Taga Shrine''' (たがじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Kamitaga, {{ill|lt=Atami|Q653364|WD=Q653364|1=Atami}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}. === Enshrined deities === The current enshrined deities are the following two, the same as at {{ill|lt=Shimotaga Shrine|Q119929622|WD=Q119929622|1=Shimotaga Shrine}} in the Shimotaga district[http://izutaga.jp/menu.php?obj=153&now=1766 Taga town walk] - Taga Tourism Association. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi|WD=Q813858}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami|WD=Q682306}} === History === Its founding period is unknown. However, because it is identified with the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} "Shiranami-no-Minaawa-no-Mikoto Shrine" of the {{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}, it is presumed to have already existed by at least the {{ill|lt=Engi|Q1374325|WD=Q1374325|1=Engi (era)}} era ({{ill|lt=901|Q23580|WD=Q23580|1=901}}-{{ill|lt=923|Q30108|WD=Q30108|1=923}})Shizuoka Prefecture Japanese History Education Research Group (ed.), 'Historical Walks of Shizuoka Prefecture (Historical Walk 22),' Yamakawa Publishing, 2006, p.8. In the {{ill|lt=precincts|Q11429002|WD=Q11429002|1=keidai}}, an ancient {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=ritual site|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Kamitaga Miyawaki Site|Q109850263|WD=Q109850263|1=Kamitaga Miyawaki Site}}) has been found, suggesting it had been treated as a sacred place in some form since before the Engi era. This shrine also performs the "{{ill|lt=Kashima Odori|Q11677163|WD=Q11677163|1=kashima odo}}" dance that traces its origin to {{ill|lt=Kashima Jingū|Q706499|WD=Q706499|1=Kashima Jingū}}. === Precinct === File:Taga-jinja (Atami), shaden.jpg|Shrine buildings File:Taga-jinja (Atami), haiden.jpg|Haiden File:Taga-jinja (Atami), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Taga-jinja (Atami), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Taga-jinja (Atami), iwakura-1.jpg|Ritual site ({{ill|lt=Kamitaga Miyawaki Site|Q109850263|WD=Q109850263|1=Kamitaga Miyawaki Site}}) File:Taga-jinja (Atami), torii-1.jpg|Torii === Cultural properties === *Taga Shrine {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kashima Odori|1=UNKNOWN}} (Atami City designated intangible folk cultural property)[https://www.city.atami.lg.jp/shisei/toukei/1001292/1001293.html List of cultural properties in Atami] Atami City === Festivals === * Grand annual festival (July 28-29) ** July 28 - Eve festival rites, divine procession, Kashima dance, floats and mikoshi[https://atamii.jp/today/上多賀に祭り囃子-宵宮祭で多賀神社例大祭始まる/ Festival music in Kamitaga: Taga Shrine grand festival begins with eve procession] - Atami Net News, 2019/7/29. ** July 29 - Main festival rites, divine procession, Kashima dance, hōren beach-descent ceremony (Tomata Port), return procession festival, floats and mikoshi[https://atamii.jp/today/114418/ Taga Shrine grand festival: magnificent divine procession closes Atami summer festivals] - Atami Net News, 2019/7/30. === Access === *10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Izu-Taga Station|Q11380555|WD=Q11380555|1=Izu-Taga Station}}. *{{ill|lt=Tōkai Bus|Q11527778|WD=Q11527778|1=Tokai Bus}} : "Miyawaki" bus stop === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === *Shizuoka Prefecture Japanese History Education Research Group (ed.), 'Historical Walks of Shizuoka Prefecture (Historical Walk 22),' Yamakawa Publishing, 2006 === External links === * {{Commonscat-inline|Taga-jinja (Atami)}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Taga Shrine (Atami)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine (Atami)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Taga Shrine (Atami)]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shizuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:熱海市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings in Atami]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izu Province]] [[Category:Taga Shrines]]
